perturbation.original_text: (a) “Confidential Information” shall include all personally identifiable student information protected under the Family Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA), California Education Code 49073.1, and NUSD Board Policy 5125.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) “Confidential Information” may include personally identifiable student information.
perturbation.explanation: This removes the explicit mention and protection afforded by FERPA, California Education Code, and NUSD policy, weakening the protection of student data. It implies that protection is discretionary rather than mandatory.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA)
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification weakens the explicit acknowledgment and adherence to FERPA regulations, potentially leading to non-compliance with federal law concerning the privacy of student records. FERPA mandates specific protections for student educational records, and this change makes it seem optional.
perturbation.justification: The original contract explicitly includes specific regulations (FERPA, California Education Code, and NUSD Board Policy) to protect student information. The changed text weakens this protection by making it discretionary, which contradicts the mandatory nature of FERPA regulations as explained in the Law Explanation. The scraped snippet 1 mentions "Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act of 1974 93-380 , title V, Sec. 513, 1974-08-21, 88 Stat. 571 ( 20 U.S.C. 1221 note)" indicating its legal basis, and the modification makes it seem optional, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: DISCLOSEE shall, upon request, provide opportunity for NUSD, or the owner of information such as a student or student’s parent/guardian, to review, update, and or correct erroneous information contained in any pupil records. DISCLOSEE shall assist NUSD in the notification of students and parents/guardians in the event of unauthorized disclosure of pupil records.
perturbation.changed_text: DISCLOSEE may provide an opportunity for NUSD to review information contained in pupil records. DISCLOSEE may assist NUSD in the notification of students and parents/guardians in the event of unauthorized disclosure of pupil records.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'may' makes the review, update, and correction of student records discretionary. Removing the explicit inclusion of the student or their parent/guardian further weakens their rights to access and correct their own information.  This also makes assisting in notification of a data breach discretionary.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Education Code Section 49069 grants students and parents the right to access and challenge the content of their school records. By changing 'shall' to 'may' and removing direct reference to students/parents, the agreement contradicts this legally mandated right to access and correct records.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, EDC 49069 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text
perturbation.justification: The change from "shall" to "may" regarding the provision of opportunity for review and correction of student records, combined with the removal of explicit mention of students/parents, contradicts the principle of student/parental rights to access and challenge school records, as indicated by the Law Explanation. While Scraped Law Snippet 2 is missing, Scraped Law Snippet 1 points to California Education Code Section 49069, which, according to the Law Explanation, grants these rights. The original text ensures these rights are actively upheld, whereas the modified text makes them discretionary, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: DISCLOSEE acknowledges that monetary damages may not be a sufficient remedy for unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information and that NUSD shall be entitled, without waiving any other rights or remedies, to such injunctive or equitable relief as may be deemed proper by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: DISCLOSEE acknowledges that monetary damages may not be a sufficient remedy for unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the section that NUSD is entitled, without waiving any other rights or remedies, to such injunctive or equitable relief as may be deemed proper by a court of competent jurisdiction, limits NUSD recourse.  This limits NUSD's options should the agreement be broken.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Civil Code § 3422 outlines the conditions under which injunctive relief is available. By removing the statement that NUSD is entitled to injunctive relief, the agreement potentially contradicts the availability of this remedy under California law, especially in cases of trade secret misappropriation or breach of confidentiality.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 3422 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 4. GENERAL PROVISIONS [3274 - 9566] ( Heading of Division 4 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 16. ) PART 1. RELIEF [3274 - 3428] ( Part 1 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 3. SPECIFIC AND PREVENTIVE RELIEF [3366 - 3424] ( Title 3 enacted 1872. ) CHAPTER 3. Preventive Relief [3420 - 3424] ( Chapter 3 enacted 1872. ) 3422. Except where otherwise provided by this Title, a final injunction may be granted to prevent the breach of an obligation existing in favor of the applicant: 1. Where pecuniary compensation would not afford adequate relief; 2. Where it would be extremely difficult to ascertain the amount of compensation which would afford adequate relief; 3. Where the restraint is necessary to prevent a multiplicity of judicial proceedings; or, 4. Where the obligation arises from a trust. (Enacted 1872.)
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes NUSD's explicit entitlement to injunctive or equitable relief, which the original contract provides for. This contradicts the principle outlined in California Civil Code § 3422, as shown in Scraped Law Snippet 1. The snippet states that 'a final injunction may be granted to prevent the breach of an obligation existing in favor of the applicant' when 'pecuniary compensation would not afford adequate relief'. By removing the explicit mention of injunctive relief, the modified contract weakens NUSD's ability to seek such relief, especially in cases where monetary damages are insufficient, creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The DISCLOSEE acknowledges that it shall not take any action that may cause the DISCLOSEE or NUSD to be out of compliance with FERPA, California Education Code 49073.1, or NUSD Board Policy 5125.
perturbation.changed_text: The DISCLOSEE acknowledges that it shall use reasonable efforts not to cause the DISCLOSEE or NUSD to be out of compliance.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the explicit references to FERPA, California Education Code, and NUSD Board Policy significantly weakens the commitment to compliance. Changing 'shall not take any action' to 'shall use reasonable efforts' lowers the standard of care required, creating potential for non-compliance and legal risk.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA) - Compliance Requirements
perturbation.law_explanation: FERPA (34 CFR Part 99) mandates strict compliance regarding the handling and protection of student records. By changing the language to 'reasonable efforts', the agreement implies that full compliance is not guaranteed, which directly contradicts the stringent requirements of FERPA.

perturbation.justification: The original text ensures strict compliance with FERPA, California Education Code, and NUSD Board Policy. The changed text weakens this commitment by stating the DISCLOSEE shall use 'reasonable efforts' to comply, implying that full compliance is not guaranteed. This directly contradicts FERPA's mandate for strict compliance, as highlighted in the Law Explanation ('By changing the language to 'reasonable efforts', the agreement implies that full compliance is not guaranteed, which directly contradicts the stringent requirements of FERPA.'). While Scraped Law Snippet 2 is missing, Scraped Law Snippet 1 references the Code of Federal Regulations (eCFR :: 34 CFR Part 99 -- Family Educational Rights and Privacy), which reinforces FERPA's regulatory basis. The original text's omission of specific regulatory requirements creates a loophole, therefore falling under the definition of Omission Legal Contradiction.
